metadata.description: EtherFi is a liquid staking protocol that issues eETH (and its wrapped form weETH) in exchange for ETH deposits, which are then staked on the Beacon Chain and restaked via EigenLayer. The protocol's core contracts are upgradeable through an immutable EtherFiTimelock with a 3-day delay, ultimately controlled by a 3/5 team multisig. EtherFi relies on EigenLayer for restaking, Lido's stETH for liquidity operations, Curve for price feeds, and LayerZero for cross-chain bridging. The protocol manages $21.03K in direct capital and $6.62B in eETH token value.

admins.name: StakingManager (Contract)
admins.description: Internal contract that manages validator lifecycle — updates auction bid information, links pubkeys to nodes, processes auction fee transfers, and controls the UpgradeableBeacon for EtherFiNode implementations. Also mints and burns TNFTs. Upgradeable and owned by EtherFiTimelock.

admins.name: EtherFiNodesManager (Contract)
admins.description: Internal contract that manages EtherFiNode operations across 28 permissioned functions — creates EigenPods, submits proofs, queues ETH withdrawals, delegates to EigenLayer operators, and distributes auction revenue. Also burns TNFTs on withdrawal. Upgradeable and owned by EtherFiTimelock.

admins.name: EtherFi Timelock (3 days)
admins.description: Immutable timelock contract with a 3-day (72h) minimum delay. Controls 152 permissioned functions across virtually all core protocol contracts, including upgradeTo on LiquidityPool, WithdrawRequestNFT, StakingManager, EtherFiNodesManager, Liquifier, MembershipManager, and more. Can transfer ownership, update admin addresses, configure oracle committee members, and modify protocol parameters. Holds $1.58B in direct capital exposure and $6.62B in token value at risk.

admins.name: EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH (Contract)
admins.description: Internal contract that handles cross-chain ETH sync via LayerZero. Can unwrap L2 ETH through the Liquifier and mint DummyTokenUpgradeable tokens for cross-chain accounting. Upgradeable, owned by EtherFiTimelock with a separate ProxyAdmin.

admins.name: Zero Address (Renounced)
admins.description: The zero address holds renounced permissions: rescueERC20 on WrapTokenV3ETH and cbETH, acceptOwnership on RoleRegistry, and mint on DummyTokenUpgradeable. These functions are effectively unexercisable.

admins.name: EtherFi Team 3/5 Multisig
admins.description: A 3-of-5 Gnosis Safe multisig that controls the EtherfiOFTAdapterUpgradeable contract (LayerZero OFT bridge adapter) — can set rate limits, grant/revoke roles, and configure peers. Also manages roles on multiple DummyTokenUpgradeable contracts used for cross-chain sync. This multisig is the terminal owner identified in the governance traversal.

admins.name: EtherFiAdmin (Contract)
admins.description: Internal admin contract that executes protocol fee payments, adds locked ETH amounts for withdrawals, and processes deposits on LiquidityPool. Also triggers rebase on MembershipManager. Controls $46.94M in reachable capital. Owned by EtherFiTimelock.

admins.name: EtherFiRedemptionManager (Contract)
admins.description: Internal protocol contract that can transfer stETH from EtherFiRestaker and execute withdrawals and eETH share burns on LiquidityPool. Controls $1.58B in direct capital. Upgradeable, with proxy admin set to the zero address (non-upgradeable proxy admin).

admins.name: LiquidityPool (Contract)
admins.description: Internal protocol contract that controls eETH minting and burning via mintShares and burnShares on the eETH token contract, processes withdrawal requests on WithdrawRequestNFT, and creates/registers/funds Beacon validators on StakingManager. This is a contract-to-contract permission, not a human admin — the LiquidityPool itself is upgradeable and owned by EtherFiTimelock.

admins.name: MembershipManager (Contract)
admins.description: Internal contract that triggers LiquidityPool rebase, requests membership NFT withdrawals, and executes LP withdrawals. Controls $46.94M in reachable capital. Upgradeable and owned by EtherFiTimelock.

admins.name: RoleRegistry (Contract)
admins.description: Internal role-based access control contract that governs WithdrawRequestNFT operations (finalizeRequests, invalidateRequest, validateRequest, pause/unpause) and Wrapped eETH Token recovery and upgrades. Controls $0 in token value. The RoleRegistry itself is upgradeable and owned by EtherFiTimelock.

dependencies.name: Ethereum Beacon Chain Deposit Contract
dependencies.description: Immutable Ethereum deposit contract used by StakingManager and LiquidityPool to fund Beacon Chain validators via the deposit function. Essential for the protocol's core staking operation.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer EigenPodManager
dependencies.description: EigenLayer's pod management contract used to create EigenPods, look up pod ownership, and track delegated share changes. Called by StakingManager during validator setup and by EtherFiNodesManager for restaking operations. Used by 32 functions.

dependencies.name: LayerZero Endpoint Owner
dependencies.description: External owner of the LayerZero EndpointV2 contract. Can register libraries, set default send/receive libraries, and transfer ownership. Not controlled by EtherFi.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer DelegationManager
dependencies.description: EigenLayer's delegation contract used for delegating to operators, queuing and completing withdrawals, and managing deposit shares. Central to EtherFi's restaking strategy — called by EtherFiNodesManager and EtherFiRestaker. $1.58B in funds at risk across 24 calling functions.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer StrategyManager
dependencies.description: EigenLayer's strategy contract used to deposit stETH into restaking strategies via depositIntoStrategy. Called by EtherFiRestaker. $1.54B in funds at risk.

dependencies.name: Lido stETH Token
dependencies.description: Lido's liquid staking token used by Liquifier and EtherFiRestaker for allowance checks, balance queries, and approvals. Core to the protocol's stETH liquidity operations. $1.58B in funds at risk.

dependencies.name: EigenLayer RewardsCoordinator
dependencies.description: EigenLayer's rewards distribution contract. EtherFiRestaker calls setClaimerFor to configure reward claiming. $1.54B in funds at risk.

dependencies.name: Lido WithdrawalQueueERC721
dependencies.description: Lido's withdrawal queue used by EtherFiRestaker to request and claim stETH withdrawals. Called across 6 functions for managing stETH-to-ETH conversion.

dependencies.name: Curve WrapTokenV3ETH (wstETH/ETH)
dependencies.description: Curve's wrapped stETH token contract used by Liquifier for totalSupply, exchange rate queries, and permit operations. Provides pricing data for stETH wrapping/unwrapping.

dependencies.name: Curve Pool (view-only)
dependencies.description: Curve AMM pool used by Liquifier for get_dy price quotes. View-only dependency — no state-changing calls.

dependencies.name: Coinbase cbETH Token
dependencies.description: Coinbase's wrapped staked ETH token. Liquifier reads its exchangeRate for cbETH pricing in the liquification process.

dependencies.name: LayerZero EndpointV2
dependencies.description: LayerZero's immutable messaging endpoint used by EtherfiOFTAdapterUpgradeable and EtherfiL1SyncPoolETH for cross-chain eETH/weETH bridging. Calls include setDelegate, send, sendCompose, and burn.

funds.name: LiquidityPool (ETH)
funds.description: Core liquidity pool holding $46.94M primarily in ETH from user deposits awaiting validator allocation or withdrawal processing. This is the main entry and exit point for ETH in the protocol.

funds.name: EtherFiRestaker (EigenLayer Positions)
funds.description: Holds $1.54B in EigenLayer restaking positions, split between stETH deposits on EigenLayer ($1.23B) and Lido staking positions ($311.65M). This is the largest capital concentration in the protocol.

funds.name: eETH Token
funds.description: The protocol's liquid staking receipt token with a market cap of $6.62B. Represents claims on staked ETH held by the protocol.

funds.name: weETH Token (Wrapped eETH)
funds.description: Wrapped version of eETH with a market cap of (N/A). An ERC-20 wrapper that accrues staking rewards by increasing its exchange rate against eETH.

funds.name: Treasury (ETH)
funds.description: Protocol treasury holding $21.03K in ETH. Immutable contract owned by EtherFiTimelock, used for protocol revenue collection.

codeAndAudits.title: Code & Audits
codeAndAudits.description: Smart contract analysis and audit history
